laws.actName: Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, 1971
laws.title: Insult to Indian National Flag and Constitution of India
laws.description: Whoever in any public place or within public view burns, mutilates, defaces, defiles, disfigures, destroys, tramples upon or otherwise shows disrespect to or brings into contempt the Indian National Flag or the Constitution of India by words or acts shall be punished.
laws.simpleExplanation: If someone disrespects or insults the Indian National Flag or Constitution in public, they can be punished.

punishments.description: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.actName: Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, 1971
laws.title: Prevention of singing of National Anthem
laws.description: Whoever intentionally prevents the singing of the Indian National Anthem or causes disturbance to an assembly engaged in such singing shall be punished.
laws.simpleExplanation: If someone stops or disturbs people singing the National Anthem, they can be punished.

punishments.description: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.actName: Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, 1971
laws.title: Minimum penalty on second or subsequent offence
laws.description: Whoever, having already been convicted of an offence under section 2 or section 3, is again convicted of such offence shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term not less than one year.
laws.simpleExplanation: If a person repeats the offence after being convicted once, they must be punished with at least one year of imprisonment.

punishments.description: Imprisonment not less than one year for repeat offences.
